key_insight: Physics-augmented neural networks can be embedded as user material models in explicit finite element codes to capture hyperelastic behavior with machine-learning flexibility and physics consistency.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical deployment of data-driven constitutive laws in Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss for impact simulations without requiring custom solvers.
method: Train PANN hyperelastic models in PyTorch (or similar frameworks) with embedded physical constraints.
Transfer pretrained network architecture and weights into autogenerated Fortran user material routines.
Optimize inference cost by changing activation functions, e.g., SoftPlus to SQuarePlus.
evidence_signal: An example impact simulation in the paper shows the generated PANN user material reproducing the nonlinear large-strain response characteristic of hyperelastic materials.
watch_item: Deployment depends on correct translation of the trained network into efficient user material code, and performance can be sensitive to network architecture and activation choice.
paper_title: Implementation of Hyperelastic Physics-Augmented Neural Networks in the Explicit Finite Element Codes Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss with Applications to Impact Events
